critical_invariants: Page table structure must follow x86-64 4-level paging format (PML4 -> PDPT -> PD -> PT)
Identity mapping of first 4GB must be preserved for kernel addressing
CR3 must point to a valid, aligned PML4 base address
Page table entries must have correct Present, Read/Write, and User/Supervisor bits
NX (No-Execute) bit usage must remain consistent for security
Write-protect (WP) bit in CR0 must be set after page table initialization
known_opportunities: TLB flush optimization: use INVLPG for single-page invalidation instead of full CR3 reload
Page walk reduction: promote 4KB pages to 2MB large pages where contiguous
Reduce page table setup loop iterations by unrolling
Use REP STOSQ for bulk page table zeroing instead of manual loops
PCID (Process Context ID) support to avoid TLB flushes on context switch
Global page bit (PGE) for kernel pages to survive CR3 reloads
